steps.title: Attach the that-clause
steps.content: The words that often incorporate introduce a restrictive relative clause modifying scribbles. That kind of clause should follow the noun directly, with no punctuation separating the noun from the clause.

steps.title: Reject fragmenting marks
steps.content: A uses a semicolon before a dependent that-clause. C uses a colon where no independent clause introduces an explanation. D uses a period and leaves the that-clause as a fragment. The answer is B.
